However, management expects film-based stores to become more popular and requires at least 5 film-based stores to be opened. Film-based stores can on average process 2 orders every hour while an electrical-based store can process on average four orders every hour. Film-based stores requires 10 units of electricity every hour while the electric-based stores take 15 units of electricity every hour. Further, the photography firm wants to be able to make at least 170 orders per hour and use at most 600 units of electricity every hour. How many of each store should be opened to minimize the total number of stores?

constraints.description: electrical-based stores at least two of film-based stores

name: A nutritionist recommends that a child eat fruits and drink ...
description: A nutritionist recommends that a child eat fruits and drink juice to tackle a certain disease. Each serving of fruit contains 5 units of the necessary mineral and 3 units of hydration. Each serving of juice contains 7 units of the necessary mineral and 6 units of hydration. According to the nutritionist, the child must intake at least 60 units of the necessary mineral every week and 40 units of hydration for their body to better absorb the mineral into their body. Further, each serving of fruit contains 9 units of sugar and each serving of juice contains 14 units of sugar. Since juice is more convenient, the child wants to take at least twice as many servings of juice as fruits. How many of each should the child be served to minimize the total sugar intake?

constraints.description: juice at least twice of fruits

name: A patient lacking sleep can take two drowsiness medications,...
description: A patient lacking sleep can take two drowsiness medications, sleeping pills, and sleep supplement. Per serving, sleeping pills increase sleepiness by 2 units and introduces 0.6 units of discomfort. Per serving, sleep supplement increases sleepiness by 3.5 units and introduces 0.3 units of discomfort. Furthermore, pills contain 1 unit of sugar while sleep supplement contains 2.5 units of sugar per serving. The patient needs at least 10 units of sleepiness and can take at most 2.5 units of discomfort. How many servings of each should the patient take to minimize his sugar intake?

name: A fitness trainer meal-preps two types of meals, a salmon lu...
description: A fitness trainer meal-preps two types of meals, a salmon lunch and a bison burger. The salmon lunch contains 15 grams of protein, 20 grams of carbs, and 8 grams of fat. The bison burger contains 30 grams of protein, 20 grams of carbs, and 13 grams of fat. The fitness trainer wants to get at least 160 grams of protein and 180 grams of carbs. In addition, because the salmon lunch is expensive, at most 25% of the meals should be salmon lunch. How many of each meal should he eat if he wants to minimize his fat intake?

name: A physics class is performing a volcano experiment using two...
description: A physics class is performing a volcano experiment using two mixtures, mixture A and mixture B. Mixture A uses 3 units of baking soda, 5 units of vinegar, and produces 2 units of eruption. Mixture B uses 4 units of baking soda, 7 units of vinegar, and produces 4 units of eruption. However, mixture A generates 2 units of garbage and mixture B generates 1 unit of garbage. The physics class has available 200 units of baking soda and 300 units of vinegar. If at most 60 units of garbage can be generated, how many of each type of mixture should be used to maximize the total amount of eruption produced?

name: A smoothie company offers delivery and ships in specialty de...
description: A smoothie company offers delivery and ships in specialty delivery and medium bags. A specialty delivery bag can hold 12 orders while a medium bag can hold 7 orders. Most delivery drivers prefer medium bags, so at least three times as many medium bags must be used as specialty delivery bags. The smoothie company has at most 45 medium bags and 60 specialty delivery bags. If the company must send at least 6 specialty delivery bags and can send at most 50 bags in total, how many of each should they send to maximize the total number of smoothies that can be delivered?

constraints.expression: medium bags ≥ three times * specialty delivery bags
constraints.description: medium bags at least three times of specialty delivery bags

name: A children clothing manufacturer can either implement small ...
description: A children clothing manufacturer can either implement small or large production lines. A small production line is capable of creating 150 sets of clothing every day and takes 12 employees to run. Conversely, the children clothing manufacturer may implement a large production line which requires 26 employees to create 500 sets of clothing per day. There are 435 employees and the manufacturer must meet the demand of at least 6300 sets of children clothing every day. How many of each production line type should be put in place to decrease the total number of production lines?

name: A lumber company owns and can use at most 450 acres of land ...
description: A lumber company owns and can use at most 450 acres of land where they can transport the lumber out using large and small logging trucks. Per acre of land, small logging trucks can be used to transport 3 units of wood. Per acre of land, a large logging truck can transport 7 units of wood. However, a small logging truck produces 12 units of carbon dioxide and requires 7 units of gasoline per acre. A large logging truck produces 25 units of carbon dioxide and requires 12 units of gasoline per acre. The lumbering company has access to 3200 units of gasoline and the company has vowed to limiting pollution to 5200 units of carbon dioxide. How should the two types of logging trucks be distributed to stay within the limits of carbon dioxide while maximizing the amount of wood transported?

name: An airport can transport travelers using commercial planes a...
description: An airport can transport travelers using commercial planes and private planes. A commercial plane can carry 180 travelers while a private plane can carry 30 travelers. Because the destination is more remote, there are few airport runways. Therefore, there must be twice as many private planes as commercial planes. If the airport needs to transport at least 3500 travelers, how many of each type of planes should be used to minimize the total number of planes used.

constraints.description: private planes must be twice of commercial planes

name: A house painting company can transport their paint in large ...
description: A house painting company can transport their paint in large cans or small cans. Large cans can hold 6 litres of paint while small cans can hold 2 litres of paint. Since small cans can be used up quickly and thus, not dry up as easily, the company wants to have at most 30% of the cans be large cans. However, there has to be at least 20 large cans especially for the basic colours. If the company needs to transport at least 120 litres of paint, how many of each size of cans should be transported to minimize the total number of cans?

name: A strawberry farmer delivers his strawberries in two types o...
description: A strawberry farmer delivers his strawberries in two types of containers, a box and a bucket. A box contains 7 units of strawberries whereas a bucket contains 9 units of strawberries. The farmer has access to 500 boxes and 300 buckets. Buckets sell for more profit; therefore, the farmer wants to sell at least three times more buckets than boxes. Additionally, he can transport at most 650 containers and at least 25 must be boxes. How many of each type of container should the farmer order to maximize the total amount of strawberries he can transport?

constraints.description: buckets at least three times of boxes

name: A supermarket can purchase two types of refrigerators to pro...
description: A supermarket can purchase two types of refrigerators to properly store their food, refrigerator ABC and refrigerator XYZ, and they have different specifications to consider. Refrigerator ABC is larger and can store 120 items but uses 3 kWh. Refrigerator XYZ is smaller more efficient and can store 80 items but uses 1 kWh. Due to soaring electric prices, the store is limited to at most 55 kWh but must store at least 1500 items. The store must have at least 8 refrigerator ABC’s and 5 refrigerator XYZ’s. How many of each type of refrigerator should the supermarket purchase to minimize the number of refrigerators needed?

name: An MOA checks a patient’s eye pressure one-by-one either by ...
description: An MOA checks a patient’s eye pressure one-by-one either by using a tonometer or a “puff of air” test. The tonometer test takes 0.05 hours to make a reading while the ”puff of air” test takes 0.02 hours to make a reading. Since the tonometer is more standard, the number of tonometer tests must be larger than the number of “puff of air” tests. Since a lot of these patients are low risk here for a check-up, at least 30 patients should have their eye pressure checked using the “puff of air” test. If the office is open for 8 hours, maximize the number of patients whose temperature can be taken?

name: A dental firm hires two types of employees: dentists and ora...
description: A dental firm hires two types of employees: dentists and oral hygienists. Dentists work 12 hours every shift and oral hygienists work 5 hours every shift. Additionally, dentists are paid $900 per shift while oral hygienists are paid $250 per shift. It is close to Halloween and the dental firm expects more patients coming in. They require 1000 hours of labour. There must be at least 20 shifts of dentist to keep the office running. If there is a budget of $65000, how many of each type of employee should the firm schedule to work to minimize the total number of shifts?

name: Banking firm ABC can build remote and urban branches to expa...
description: Banking firm ABC can build remote and urban branches to expand their reach to customers. A remote branch expects 5 new account registrations per day and requires 2 registration experts. An urban branch generally opens accounts for 12 new account registrations every day and requires 7 registration experts. The bank has available 550 registration experts and wants to open at least 1200 new accounts every day. How many of each type of branch should banking firm ABC open to minimize the total number of branches needed?

name: A milk company is delivering milk to their local families’ h...
description: A milk company is delivering milk to their local families’ homes directly either through bikes or by car. A worker on a bike can deliver 12 orders of milk and takes 40 minutes per trip. A worker on a car can deliver 50 orders of milk and takes 30 minutes per trip. The milk company needs to deliver at least 850 orders of milk. Additionally, at least 70% of delivery trips must be by bike to reduce the pollution. How many of each type of delivery trips must be scheduled to minimize the minutes per trip?

name: A logging company ships their lumber to a nearby country usi...
description: A logging company ships their lumber to a nearby country using logging trucks and by lumber carrier vessels. Each logging truck costs the logging company $250 for every trip and moves 1000 lbs of lumber. A lumber carrier vessel costs $1000 every trip and moves 6000 lbs of lumber. Due to limited waters and difficulties in scheduling the trips for lumber carrier vessels, the company’s management asks that the number of logging trucks must exceed the number of lumber carrier vessels. The logging company must transport at least 300000 lbs of lumber to the nearby country. How many of each type of transportation should the company schedule to move their lumber to minimize the total cost?

name: A tutoring company hires full-time tutors and supplements wi...
description: A tutoring company hires full-time tutors and supplements with assistant tutors from a nearby high school. Every day, full-time tutors work 8 hours and get paid $650 while assistant tutors work 4 hours and get paid $120. During Christmas break, the tutoring company expects more interest and plans to require 1200 hours of teaching availability. If the tutoring company has a budget of $55000, how many tutors and assistant tutors should be hired to minimize the amount of staff?

name: A nurse suggests that his patient drink more protein shakes ...
description: A nurse suggests that his patient drink more protein shakes and milk to meet their calcium and protein requirements. One glass of protein shake contains 3 units of calcium, 2 units of protein, and 3 grams of sugar. One glass of milk contains 5 units of calcium, 1 unit of protein, and 5 grams of sugar. The patient must get at least 90 units of calcium and 85 units of protein. Since the patent prefers milk, they must drink at least 3 times as many glasses of milk as glasses of protein shakes. How many glasses of each should they drink to minimize their sugar intake?

constraints.description: milk at least 3 times of protein shakes

name: A food distributer can either move their product using truck...
description: A food distributer can either move their product using trucks or boats. A truck can take 30 units of product and produces 80 units of pollution per trip whereas boats take 50 units of product and produces 60 units of pollution per trip. The food distributer needs to move 1000 units of product and must have a maximum of 40% of the trips using boats due to limitations of sea transportation regulations. Additionally, there must be at least 10 trips using a truck. How many of each form of transportation should be used to minimize the total amount of pollution produced?

name: A physics teacher shows her students two experiments, a forc...
description: A physics teacher shows her students two experiments, a force experiment and a hydraulics experiment. In the force experiment, 5 units of the solid and 2 units of the gas mixes and reacts to create 3 units of excitement. In the hydraulics experiment, 7 units of the solid and 6 units of the gas mixes and reacts to create 5 units of excitement. In addition, the hydraulics experiment produces 2 units of heat while the force experiment produces 3 units of heat.  The lab has available 90 units of the solid and 85 units of the gas. If at most 15 units of heat can be produced, how many experiments of each should be done to maximize the total excitement?

name: An athlete drinks protein shakes in the morning before a wor...
description: An athlete drinks protein shakes in the morning before a workout and is planning for the coming month. She has two selections of protein shakes, chocolate powder or vanilla powder shakes. Each scoop of chocolate powder protein shake contains 20 grams of protein and 4 grams of sugar. Each scoop of vanilla powder protein shake contains 18 grams of protein and 7 grams of sugar. Since she prefers the vanilla flavour, she would like to drink at least twice as many vanilla powder shakes as chocolate powder shakes. However, she must also drink at least 2 scoops of the chocolate powder shake. To avoid high blood glucose levels, she is limiting her consumption to at most 500 grams of sugar over this period of time. How many of each protein shake should the athlete drink to maximize her total protein intake over this period of time?

objective.expression: 18*vanilla powder protein shake + 20*chocolate powder protein shake

constraints.expression: 4*chocolate powder protein shake + 7*vanilla powder protein shake ≤ 500
vanilla powder ≥ twice * chocolate powder shakes
constraints.description: vanilla powder at least twice of chocolate powder shakes

name: A sample can be analyzed by two machines for their chemical ...
description: A sample can be analyzed by two machines for their chemical make-up, Analyzer 3000 and QuickAnalytics. Analyzer 3000 analyzes 20 samples of water and 30 samples of dirt per hour. QuickAnalytics examines 40 samples of water and 10 samples of dirt per hour. In addition, Analyzer 3000 creates 3.5 units of heat per hour while QuickAnalytics creates 4.5 units of heat per hour. If at least 1000 samples of water and 800 samples of dirt must be analyzed, how many hours should each machine be used to minimize the total amount of heat produced?

name: Professor ABC sells his economics textbook as paperback or h...
description: Professor ABC sells his economics textbook as paperback or hardcover versions. Paperback books yield a profit of $150 USD whereas hardcover versions yield a profit of $200 USD. Since hardcover versions are more sturdy and contain some extra supplementary materials, there must be at least three times as many hardcover than paperback textbooks. However, because paperback books are less expensive, at least 100 paperback versions must be created. If the professor sells at least 1000 textbooks every year, how many of each version should he request the publisher to print to maximize the profit?

constraints.description: hardcover at least three times of paperback textbooks

name: A university has a car building fundraiser where students bu...
description: A university has a car building fundraiser where students build toy cars and toy trucks using wheels and wood and sell to benefit a nearby shelter. A toy car requires 4 wheels and 7 units of wood while a toy truck requires 6 wheels and 10 units of wood. Each team has at most 40 wheels and 75 units of wood. In addition, each team can build at most 3 toy cars and the number of toy trucks must be larger than the number toy cars. If a toy car can fundraise $30 and a toy truck can fundraise $35, how many of each toy should a team build to maximize the total amount fundraised?

name: Architects ABC has a budget of one hundred thousand dollars ...
description: Architects ABC has a budget of one hundred thousand dollars to spend every week on their employees. They hire architects and drafters to help with their clients’ projects. An architect averages 3000 dollars every week and can complete 2 projects every week. A drafter does not have all the certifications and requires an architect for supervision at all times. Therefore, there must be more architects than drafters. Additionally, a drafter averages 1200 dollars every week and completes 1 project every week. How many architects and drafters should be contracted to maximize the number of projects completed every week?

constraints.description: Linear constraint budget one hundred thousand

name: A lotion company has 1000 units of emulsifiers that are used...
description: A lotion company has 1000 units of emulsifiers that are used to make strong topical anesthetics and weak topical anesthetics. Each container of strong topical anesthetics takes 4 units of emulsifiers and 3 units of salicylates. A container of weak topical anesthetics requires 7 units of emulsifiers and 2 units of salicylates. Since many customers are purchasing for daily applications, at least 60% of containers made must be weak topical anesthetics. However, there must be at least 300 containers of the strong topical anesthetics. How many of each container should be made to minimize the total amount of salicylates required?

name: A tourism company is expecting an increase in tourists after...
description: A tourism company is expecting an increase in tourists after a pandemic and expects 10000 customers in the coming month. The tourism company can either use a minivan which can transport 6 tourists and profits the company $300 each time. They can also use a tourism bus which can transport 30 tourists and profits the company $1800 each time. There must be at least 10 tourism buses in the coming month because many come in large groups. Additionally, due to the close proximity to the tour guide, the tourism company plans to have at least 45% of the trips be by minivan to improve customer service. How many trips of each type of vehicle should be scheduled to maximize the total profit for the company?

name: A manufacturing plant has the option to offer a shuttle bus ...
description: A manufacturing plant has the option to offer a shuttle bus and outsource to a taxi service. A shuttle bus costs $20 and carries 7 workers, whereas a taxi service costs $50 and carries 5 workers. The manufacturing plant has to transport at least 250 workers and at most 5 shuttle buses can be used. How many of each type of transport should be taken to minimize the total cost?

name: A government sanitization plant processes liquid waste and s...
description: A government sanitization plant processes liquid waste and solid waste in batches. Liquid waste requires 30 units of disinfectant and 20 units of bacteria. Solid waste requires 25 units of disinfectant and 15 units of bacteria. The sanitization plant has available 1500 units of disinfectant and 1000 units of bacteria. The batches of liquid waste processed must be twice the number of solid waste processed. In addition, at most 10 batches of solid waste can be processed due to storage limitations. If each liquid waste batch is the result of 10 people and each solid waste batch is the result of 3 people, how many of each should the plant process to maximize the number of people served?

constraints.description: liquid waste must be twice of solid waste

name: A researcher is transporting their arsenal of imaging system...
description: A researcher is transporting their arsenal of imaging systems using two different methods, by delivery truck and by van. Transporting by delivery truck takes 12 hours and costs $300 per trip. Transporting by van takes 10 hours and costs $200 per trip. The researcher needs to transport 20 trips worth of equipment and has a budget of $5500. Additionally, the number of transports by the delivery truck must be less than the number of transports by van. How many of each transportation method should they use to minimize the total number of hours spent on delivery?

name: A butcher sells marinated wild boar and processed lamb chops...
description: A butcher sells marinated wild boar and processed lamb chops. Each portion of marinated wild boar requires 3 minutes of preparation and 6 ounces of meat. One portion of processed lamb chops requires 1 minute of preparation and 7 ounces of meat. Since processed lamb chops are more popular and common, the butcher plans to make at least twice as many processed lamb chops as marinated wild boars. The butcher has a small staff and only has available 250 collective minutes every week to preparing these two items and only has available 1375 ounces of meat. If a portion of marinated wild boar sells for a revenue of 6 dollars whereas a portion of processed lamb chops sells for a revenue of 4 dollars, how many of each should the butcher make to maximize their revenue?

constraints.expression: processed lamb chops ≥ twice * marinated wild boars
constraints.description: processed lamb chops at least twice of marinated wild boars

name: A water purification station has a large plant and a small p...
description: A water purification station has a large plant and a small plant. The large plant purifies 30 units of water per hour and produces 20 units of by-product per hour. The small plant purifies 12 units of water per hour and produces 2 units of by-product per hour. The large plant requires 15 filters while the small plant requires 12 filters per hour. The water purification station only has available 1200 filters right now. If the station must purify at least 1200 units of water and at least 500 units of by-product, how many hours should each plant be run to minimize the total time needed?

name: A clothing manufacturing company needs to transport their pr...
description: A clothing manufacturing company needs to transport their product by cargo ship or airplane. A cargo ship can transport 40 units per trip while by airplane can transport 60 units per trip. A cargo ship produces 20 units of pollution and an airplane produces 35 units of pollution. There can be at least 6 airplane trips made and at least 25% of all trips must be made by cargo ship. The company needs to transport at least 750 units of their product. How many of each transportation should they use to minimize the total amount of pollution produced?

name: A garbage collection company uses two types of vehicles, tru...
description: A garbage collection company uses two types of vehicles, truck A and truck B. Truck A requires 3 workers while truck B requires 4 workers. Truck A can collect 40 units of garbage and truck B can collect 50 units of garbage. The garbage collection company has employed 150 workers. The company must use more truck A models than truck B models. Additionally, there must be at least 5 truck A’s and 8 truck B’s. How many of each type of vehicles should the company use to maximize the total amount of garbage that can be collected?

name: A city’s dispatching service needs to send out firefighting ...
description: A city’s dispatching service needs to send out firefighting units for an upcoming extremely dry period. Firefighting units can either send units of firefighters or volunteer fire patrols. Firemen can put out 1000 sq ft of fire per trip and takes 10 minutes. Volunteer fire patrols can put out 800 sq ft of fire per trip and takes 12 minutes. There is a dry period of at least 8000 minutes that has to be covered and at least 10% of all units must be volunteer fire patrols due to shortages in budget. In addition, there can be at most 30 units dispatched because the city has limited fire trucks. How should the dispatcher allocate resources to each type of firefighting units to maximize the total sq ft that can be put out?

objective.expression: total sq ft that can be put out

name: A landscaping company provides services from an expert or an...
description: A landscaping company provides services from an expert or an apprentice. An expert costs the company 500 dollars for each shift and can care for 100 square meters of land whereas an apprentice costs the company 300 dollars for each shift and can care for 80 square meters of land. Because of the price to the customer, the company expects the demand of the public to want at least twice as many apprentices than experts. However, there are loyal customers that prefer the experts. Therefore, at least 10% of shifts must be expert shifts. The company must care for 5000 square meters of land. How many of each type of service should be used to minimize the total cost to the company?

constraints.description: apprentices at least twice of experts

name: A doctor can prescribe two types of medication for high gluc...
description: A doctor can prescribe two types of medication for high glucose levels, a diabetic pill and a diabetic shot. Per dose, diabetic pill delivers 1 unit of glucose reducing medicine and 2 units of blood pressure reducing medicine. Per dose, a diabetic shot delivers 2 units of glucose reducing medicine and 3 units of blood pressure reducing medicine. In addition, diabetic pills provide 0.4 units of stress and the diabetic shot provides 0.9 units of stress. At most 20 units of stress can be applied over a week and the doctor must deliver at least 30 units of glucose reducing medicine. How many doses of each should be delivered to maximize the amount of blood pressure reducing medicine delivaered to the patient?

name: There are two methods of extracting a chemical element, meth...
description: There are two methods of extracting a chemical element, method X and method Y.  Method X requires 5 atomic units (au) of aluminum, 1 au of oxygen, and can extract 0.5 au of the desired chemical element. Method Y requires 6 au of aluminum, 2 au of oxygen, and can extract 0.7 au of the desired chemical element. There are 650 au of aluminum, and 425 au of oxygen available. What is the best combination of these two methods to maximize the total amount of desired chemical element?

name: A toy storage company needs to transport toys using a manual...
description: A toy storage company needs to transport toys using a manual pallet jack or an electric pallet jack. The toy storage company has access to at most 6 manual pallet jacks and 15 electric pallet jacks. The manual pallet jacks can transport 15 boxes every hour while electric pallet jacks can transport 25 boxes every hour. Since electric jacks require less work, the number of electric pallet jacks must exceed the number of manual pallet jacks. If the company can use at most 8 manual pallet jacks and at least 5 electric pallet jacks, how many of each should the company use to maximize the number of boxes they can transport?

name: A composting company can store compost in medium containers ...
description: A composting company can store compost in medium containers and large containers. A medium container needs to be checked 5 times a week and can store 25 units of compost. A large container needs to be checked 2 times a week and can store 40 units of compost. The company needs to store at least 500 units of compost and there must be at least 3 medium containers. There must also be at least 2 large containers and the number of large containers must be three times as much as the number of medium containers. How many of each type of containers should be used to minimize the total number of times checking containers?

constraints.expression: large containers ≥ three times * medium containers
constraints.description: large containers must be three times of medium containers

name: A chemist is performing novel research to create element A u...
description: A chemist is performing novel research to create element A using two known methods: the gamma experiment and the omega experiment. The gamma experiment creates 12 units of element A and uses 3 units of electricity, 6 units of copper, and 3 minutes. The omega experiment creates 16 units of element A and uses 2 units of electricity, 9 units of copper, and 1 minutes. The chemist has available 1000 minutes, 4200 units of copper, and 1100 units of electricity. How many of each experiment should the chemist perform to maximize the total amount of element A produced?

name: A strawberry farmer has available 225 hectares of land and s...
description: A strawberry farmer has available 225 hectares of land and she needs to harvest the strawberries either through employees or U-pick. For each hectare of land, employees can hand-pick 60 lbs of strawberries which creates 5 lbs of waste, and takes 30 minutes. Conversely, for each hectare of land, opening the farm for U-pick can pick 45 lbs of strawberries which creates 20 lbs of waste, but only takes 20 minutes. The farmer has a maximum amount of people allowed on the field at all times and due to the restriction of trying to sell the product while it is still fresh, expects that at maximum capacity she only has 8000 minutes available for picking. Additionally, there can be at most 6400 lbs of waste. How many hectares should be allocated to each harvesting technique to maximize the amount of strawberries that can be picked?

name: A furniture store builds and sells sofas and beds
description: A furniture store builds and sells sofas and beds. Each sofa costs 6 units of wood and 8 units of time while each bed costs 5 units of wood and 10 units of time. The store has available 620 units of wood and 1055 units of time. Each bed takes an additional 5 hours of shipping time while each sofa takes 3 hours of shipping time. Since there are usually more sofas than beds, the store aims to build twice as many sofas than beds. How many of each should the store make to minimize the total shipping time?

constraints.description: sofas aims to build twice of beds

name: A pharmaceutical manufacturing plant has 1000 mg of cornstar...
description: A pharmaceutical manufacturing plant has 1000 mg of cornstarch to rush orders of diabetic pills and latex gloves. Each diabetic pill requires 4 mg of cornstarch and 0.2 minutes to manufacture. Each individual latex glove requires 7 mg of cornstarch and 0.15 minutes to manufacture. The pharmaceutical manufacturing plant needs to make at least 20 diabetic pills. Since latex gloves are important, at least 60% of produced items must be latex gloves. How many of each should the pharmaceutical manufacturing plant make to minimize the total number of minutes needed?

name: A popular coffee shop has coffee and cappuccinos
description: A popular coffee shop has coffee and cappuccinos. Each coffee requires 8 units of milk and 2 units of water. Each cappuccino requires 7 units of cream and 4 units of water. The coffee shop purchases a total of 3300 units of milk and 2600 units of cream. Regulars like the coffee and at least thirty percent of drinks must be coffee. However, cappuccinos make more profit, and the owner wants to sell more cappuccino than coffee. Since the town is in a water shortage, how many of each drink should be made to minimize the total amount of water needed?

name: A woman with vitamin deficiency is taking supplements from t...
description: A woman with vitamin deficiency is taking supplements from two brands, super vitamin XYZ and vitamin ABC. Super vitamin XYZ contains 2 mg of vitamin, 5 mg of fiber, and causes 2 units of drowsiness per serving. Vitamin ABC contains 1.5 mg of vitamin, 3 mg of fiber, and causes 3 units of drowsiness per serving. The woman wants to get at least 30 mg of vitamin and 55 mg of fiber. In addition, there must be at most 40% of the supplements taken from super vitamin XYZ and there must be at least 3 servings of super vitamin XYZ because it is expiring soon. How many servings of each should she take to minimize her total drowsiness.

name: An internet supplier needs to deliver routers to their custo...
description: An internet supplier needs to deliver routers to their customers when they first sign up. They can either transport using their internal drivers or outsource to external drivers. Internal drivers spend more time to help the customers set up and can deliver 20 routers every day whereas the external drivers can deliver 35 routers every day. External drivers use hybrid cars and produce 5 units of pollution while the internal drivers use gasoline cars and produce 12 units of pollution. The internet supplier needs to deliver at least 500 routers every day and internal drivers must make up at least 20% of all drivers due to their customer service. There must also be at least 5 external drivers. How many of each type of driver should the internet supplier plan to contract to minimize the total amount of pollution?

name: Emergency supplies need to be delivered to a remote area and...
description: Emergency supplies need to be delivered to a remote area and can either go by airplane or helicopter. An airplane can transport 35 units of goods and takes 30 minutes while a helicopter can transport 20 units of goods and takes 10 minutes due to easier take-off and landing. The number of helicopters must exceed the number of airplanes. Additionally, there must be at least 5 airplanes to fully utilize the landing space.  If there needs to be at least 500 units of goods transported, how many of each type of transportation should be utilized to minimize the total number of minutes needed?

name: A hospital gives pupil dilation drops using two types of dil...
description: A hospital gives pupil dilation drops using two types of dilation drops, fast-acting eye drops and slow-acting eye drops. The fast-acting eye drops take 5 minutes per patient to adjust the patient’s pupils while the slow-acting eye drops take 25 minutes per patient. Since the fast-acting eye drops are more expensive, at least twice as many patients must be dilated using the slow-acting eye drops than the fast-acting eye drops. However, at least 25 patients must be dilated with the fast-acting eye drops. If the clinic is open for 15000 minutes, maximize the number of patients that can be dilated.

constraints.expression: slow-acting eye drops ≥ twice * fast-acting eye drops
constraints.description: slow-acting eye drops at least twice of fast-acting eye drops

name: A famous chocolate shop is expecting a lot of orders because...
description: A famous chocolate shop is expecting a lot of orders because of an upcoming holiday. The chocolate shop is famous for selling two products, large boxes of chocolates, or small boxes of chocolates. A large box of chocolate has 24 individual chocolates while a small box of chocolate has 12 individual chocolates. The chocolate shop has a delivery service, and each trip, the shop owner can prepare ahead of time at most 110 large boxes of chocolates or 150 small boxes of chocolates. Due to space limitations of their truck, the truck can fit at most 90 total boxes of chocolates and because of a sale, there must be at least 10 large boxes of chocolates. However, small boxes of chocolates are less expensive and the shop owner expects to move at least three times as many small boxes of chocolates than large boxes of chocolates. How many of each size of chocolate box should the shop owner put in their truck for each trip to maximize the total number of individual chocolates transported to their customers?

objective.expression: 12*small box of chocolate + 24*large box of chocolate

constraints.description: large boxes of chocolates at least 10

constraints.expression: small boxes of chocolates ≥ three times * large boxes of chocolates
constraints.description: small boxes of chocolates at least three times of large boxes of chocolates

name: A competitive swimmer is training heavily and to do so, plan...
description: A competitive swimmer is training heavily and to do so, plans to eat steaks and chicken breasts over the coming week. Each steak contains 900 calories and 40 g of protein. Each chicken breast contains 700 calories and 20 g of protein. He prefers to eat chicken breasts and decides to eat at least 2 times as many chicken breasts as steaks over the coming week. However, he must also eat at least 10 steaks over the week. If he must consume at least 1200 g of protein over this coming week, how many steaks and chicken breasts should he eat to minimize the total amount of calories he is eating?

constraints.description: chicken breasts at least 2 times of steaks

name: A nearby lake is frequently artificially seeded with trout f...
description: A nearby lake is frequently artificially seeded with trout for local fishermen. The trout are transported using either large trucks or by boat. Transporting using large trucks can take 2000 trout and costs $300 per trip. Transporting with boat can take 1200 trout and costs $100 per trip. Since boats take extra preparation, at least 75% of trips should be by large trucks. Additionally, there can be at most 5 trips by boat. If at least 7000 trout need to be seeded into the nearby lake, how many of each type of trip should be taken to minimize the total cost?

name: In the North, a delivery service can deliver using either sl...
description: In the North, a delivery service can deliver using either sleds or snowmobile. Snowmobiles can deliver 150 packages while sleds can deliver 125 packages. Most villages do not want snowmobiles to pollute their regions. Therefore, there must be at least two times more sleds than snowmobiles. If the delivery service wants to deliver 150000 packages, minimize the total number of deliveries.

constraints.description: sleds at least two times of snowmobiles

name: A waste treatment company must remove waste using a large co...
description: A waste treatment company must remove waste using a large container or a medium container. A large container can take 5 tons of waste while a medium container can take 3.5 tons of waste. The treatment plant has available at most 40 large containers and 35 medium containers. Additionally, due to logistics in the storage warehouse, there must be at most 65 total containers and they must use at least 6 medium containers. Finally, there must be twice as many medium containers than large containers. How many of each container should they use to maximize the total amount of waste they can transport?

constraints.description: medium containers must be twice of large containers

name: A car manufacturing company needs to distribute their cars t...
description: A car manufacturing company needs to distribute their cars to their retail stores by either large trailers or small trailers. A large trailer can carry 6 cars while a small trailer can carry 4 cars. Since large trailers are harder to get a hold of, the number of small trailers used has to be at least three times the number of large trailers used. If at least 80 cars need to be transported, minimize the total number of trailers needed.

constraints.description: small trailers at least three of large trailers

name: A grocery store sushi supplier makes sushi in bulk and can p...
description: A grocery store sushi supplier makes sushi in bulk and can purchase either an all-in-one manufacturing line or a decoupled manufacturing line. The all-in-one manufacturing line is more efficient and can output 10000 orders per day while the decoupled manufacturing line can output 7000 items per day. The all-in-one manufacturing line requires 5 workers per day while the decoupled manufacturing line requires 10 workers per day. The grocery store sushi supplier must be able to satisfy at least 150000 orders per day and has allocated a budget to hire 135 workers. Since the decoupled manufacturing line is less efficient, at most 35% of the production can be decoupled manufacturing lines. However, there are some legacy factories remaining that already have decoupled manufacturing lines and about 10% of workers are trained on these older methods. Therefore, at least 10% must be decoupled manufacturing lines. How many of each manufacturing line should the sushi supplier plan for to reduce the total number of manufacturing lines?

name: A gas company can transport gas using a pipeline or by train...
description: A gas company can transport gas using a pipeline or by train. A pipeline can carry 8000 units of gas and a train can carry 2000 units of gas every day. Due to recent environmental changes, the number of trains used must be at least twice as many as the number of pipelines. If at least 1000000 units of gas needs to be transported every day, minimize the total number of pipelines and trains needed.

objective.expression: total number of pipelines and trains needed

constraints.description: trains at least twice of pipelines

name: An ice cream company sells ice cream as cones and tubs
description: An ice cream company sells ice cream as cones and tubs. A cone of ice cream contains 3 oz of ice cream while a tub contains 24 oz of ice cream.  Due to the recent wave of heat and a recent promotion, the company expects to sell at least 2 times more cones than tubs of ice cream. However, to reduce labour time and looking at previous sales, management decided to require stores to at least create 30 tubs of ice cream. If the company has the capacity to make 5000 oz of ice cream, how many orders of each should be made to maximize the total number of sales of ice cream?

objective.expression: total number of sales of ice cream

constraints.expression: cones ≥ 2 times * tubs of ice cream
constraints.description: cones at least 2 times of tubs of ice cream
tubs of ice cream at least 30

name: A commercial fishing company has available 200 square kilome...
description: A commercial fishing company has available 200 square kilometers of water that they can fish from. They can either allocate a square kilometer for fish farming or trawling. For each square kilometer of ocean, fish farming causes 8 units of pollution and nets 4 tons of fish and requires 6 workers every day. For each square kilometer of ocean, trawling causes 10 units of pollution, nets 3 tons of fish and requires 2 workers every day. Due to environmental regulations, there must be at most 1700 units of pollution and the company has available 500 workers daily. Find the proportion of lands that use each fishing technique to maximize the daily yield of fish.

name: A chemical company makes an acidic liquid and a basic liquid...
description: A chemical company makes an acidic liquid and a basic liquid using two types of techniques, reaction X and reaction Y. Reaction X can make 35 au of the acidic liquid and 75 au of the basic liquid per minute. Reaction Y can make 42 au of the acidic liquid and 35 au of the basic liquid per minute. Furthermore, reaction X requires 18 units of heat every minute while reaction Y requires 12 units of heat every minute. The chemical company has the capacity to generate at most 1400 units of heat every minute. If the chemical company needs to make at least 900 au of the acidic liquid and 1200 au of the basic liquid per minute, how many minutes should each reaction be run for to minimize the total time needed?

name: An arcade is implementing two systems, a card-based system a...
description: An arcade is implementing two systems, a card-based system and a coin-based system. A card-based system can accept 60 guests per hour. Conversely, a coin-based system can only accept 45 guests per hour. The card-based machine needs 6 units of electricity every hour while the coin-based machine needs only 4 units of electricity every hour. The arcade needs to be able to accept at least 1500 guests per hour but only has access to 145 units of electricity every hour. Since most guests prefer to use cards, there must be more card-based than coin-based machines. How many of each machine should be kept in the arcade to minimize the total number of machines in the arcade?

name: A tea shop brews two types of teas; black and green tea
description: A tea shop brews two types of teas; black and green tea. Brewing black tea takes 2 minutes and green tea 3 minutes. It takes 7 units of tea leaves and 15 units of filtered hot water to make a cup of black tea. It takes 5 units of tea leaves and 18 units of filtered hot water to make a cup of green tea. The owner of the tea shop has a deal with her distributer to acquire 700 units of tea leaves and 700 units of filtered hot water every week.  The tea shop also has a repeating customer in a Japanese restaurant to brew green tea for them. Therefore, the shop must brew at least four times more green tea than black tea. How many of each type of tea should be made to minimize the total time spent brewing tea?

constraints.description: green tea at least four of black tea

name: A construction site can use two transfer methods to move equ...
description: A construction site can use two transfer methods to move equipment using cranes or elevators. Each crane can transport 5 units of equipment and costs $2000 per hour. Each elevator can transport 2 units of equipment and costs $500 per hour. The construction site needs to transport at least 90 units of equipment and has available $30000. There must be at least three times more elevators than cranes. How many of each type of transfer method should be done to minimize the total number of transfer methods?

constraints.description: elevators at least three times of cranes

name: An engineering kids kit has two sets, a mechanical and chemi...
description: An engineering kids kit has two sets, a mechanical and chemistry kit. The mechanical kit requires 2 units of plastic, 4 units of materials and entertains a kid for 4 hours. The chemistry kit requires 3 units of plastic, 7 units of materials and entertains a kid for 2 hours. The chemistry kit however produces 8 units of waste while the mechanical kit produces 5 units of waste. The company has available 500 units of plastic and 450 units of materials. If the company has committed to producing at most 300 units of waste, how many of each type of kit should the company make to maximize the number of hours of entertainment?

name: An extremely large mall needs at least 100 transportation me...
description: An extremely large mall needs at least 100 transportation methods for customers to move up and down floors. They can either purchase an escalator or an elevator. Due to space limitations, there must be more escalators than elevators, but for convenience, there must be at least 10 elevators. An elevator can transport 1000 customers every day and uses 6 units of electricity in that time span. An escalator can transport 2400 customers every day and uses 9 units of electricity in that time span. The mall has available 800 units of electricity every day. How many of each transportation method should the mall install to maximize the number of customers they can transport every day?

name: A fitness trainer is scheduling her client to eat more fruit...
description: A fitness trainer is scheduling her client to eat more fruits and vegetables to meet their vitamin and fiber requirements. A pack of fruits contains 4 units of vitamin and 7.5 units of fiber. A pack of vegetables contains 7 units of vitamin and 5 units of fiber. The client must meet their weekly minimum requirements of 100 units of vitamin and 120 units of fiber. In addition, because fruits contain more hydration, the client must eat at least twice as many packs of fruits as packs of vegetables. If a pack of fruits contains 3 units of sugar and a pack of vegetables contains 1 unit of sugar, how many of packs of each should the client consume to minimize their sugar intake?

constraints.expression: packs of fruits ≥ twice * packs of vegetables
constraints.description: packs of fruits at least twice of packs of vegetables

name: A skiing resort has two methods of getting their customers u...
description: A skiing resort has two methods of getting their customers up to their resort. They can either use a gondola, or a large bus. On average, a gondola can transport skiers and snowboarders at a rate of 20 people per minute whereas a large bus can transport at a rate of 5 people per minute. However, gondolas cost the resort a lot to maintain and at most 40% of the transportation methods implemented can be gondolas. Since large buses are easier to implement, there must be at least ten times more large buses than gondolas. If the manufacturer wants to move at least 500 customers every minute up the mountain, find the minimum transportation implementations that can be used.

constraints.description: large buses at least ten times of gondolas

name: A dietician recommends his patient drink more juice and vita...
description: A dietician recommends his patient drink more juice and vitamin-added milk to increase her vitamin and hydration intake. Each cup of juice contains 12 units of vitamins and 8 units of hydration. Each vitamin-added milk contains 14 units of vitamins and 10 units of hydration. The patient needs to consume at least 180 units of vitamins and 140 units of hydration. Since the patient is slightly lactose-intolerant, there must be at most 10% of all cups of drinks be vitamin-added milk. If each cup of vitamin-added milk contains 3 units of sugar and each cup of juice contains 10 units of sugar, how many cups of each should she drink to minimize her sugar intake?

name: A restaurant chain can make two types of meals, a steak or v...
description: A restaurant chain can make two types of meals, a steak or vegetarian meal. The steak meal makes $18 profit each and it takes 3 minutes for a chef to make. A vegetarian meal makes $9 profit each and takes 2 minutes for a chef make. The restaurant chain needs to make at least $500 profit every day and has available 1000 minutes of chef involvement. How many of each meal should the restaurant try to push through the waiting staff to minimize the total number of meals they need to make?

name: A fishery wants to transport their catch
description: A fishery wants to transport their catch. They can either use local sled dogs or trucks. Local sled dogs can take 100 fish per trip while trucks can take 300 fish per trip. The cost per trip for sled dogs is $50 while the cost per trip for a truck is $100. The budget is at most $1000 and the number of sled dog trips must be less than the number of truck trips. Formulate an LP to maximize the number of fish that can be transported.

name: An office supply company makes two types of printers: color ...
description: An office supply company makes two types of printers: color printers and black and white printers. Different sections of the factory with different teams produce each printer. The color printer team can produce at most 20 color printers per day while the black and white printer team can produce at most 30 black and white printers per day. Both teams require use of the same paper tray installing machine and this machine can make at most 35 printers of either type each day. Color printers generate a profit of $200 per printer while black and white printers generate a profit of $70 per printer. How many of each printer should be made to maximize the company's profit?

name: An accounting firm has senior accountants earning $3000 per ...
description: An accounting firm has senior accountants earning $3000 per week and junior accountants earning $1000 per week. The contracts with companies to provide accounting services require at least 100 accountants, of whom at least 5 must be senior accountants. To make sure there is enough experience on the accounting team, the number of senior accountants should be at least a third of the number to junior accountants. The firm wants to keep the weekly wage bill below $150000. Formulate an LP to minimize the wage bill.

constraints.description: senior accountants at least third of junior accountants

name: Ben is growing apples and pears on his orchard
description: Ben is growing apples and pears on his orchard. He has 50 acres available on which he must grow a minimum of 5 acres of apples and a minimum of 10 acres of pears to meet demands. The profit per apple is $2 and the profit per pear is $4. He prefers to grow more pears than apples but limitations in his workforce allow him to grow at most twice the amount of pears as apples. How many of each fruit should Ben grow in order to maximize his profit? What is that profit?

constraints.description: pears at most twice of apples

name: A lighting company makes desk-lamps and night-lamps
description: A lighting company makes desk-lamps and night-lamps. There is an expected demand of at least 30 desk-lamps and 50 night-lamps each day. However, due to the size of their factory, they can make at most 150 desk-lamps and 180 night-lamps per day. To satisfy a contract, a minimum of 100 lamps must be made each day. If the profit per desk-lamp sold is $5 and the profit per night-lamp sold is $8, how many lamps of each type should be made to maximize profit?

name: Mr
description: Mr. Roberts want to invest in the telecom and healthcare industries. He has $100000 to invest. He has decided that the amount invested in telecom be at least three times as much as the amount invested in healthcare. But the money invested in telecom must be at most $70000. If investments in telecom earn 3% and investments in healthcare earn 1%, how much should Mr. Roberts invest in each to maximize profit?

constraints.description: telecom at least three of healthcare

name: A farmer has 140 hectares available to grow tomatoes and pot...
description: A farmer has 140 hectares available to grow tomatoes and potatoes. She prefers to plant more tomatoes than potatoes, but the soil and weather conditions allow her to grow at most twice the amount of tomatoes to that of potatoes. In addition, she must grow at least 20 hectares of tomatoes and at least 30 hectares of potatoes to meet community demands. If the profit per hectare of tomatoes is $350 and the profit per hectare of potatoes is $600, how many hectares of each crop should she plant to maximize profit? What is this profit?

constraints.description: tomatoes at most twice of potatoes

name: A audio company make two types of headphones: wired headphon...
description: A audio company make two types of headphones: wired headphones and wireless headphones. Two different teams make each type of headphones. The wired team can make at most 100 wired headphones per day and the wireless team can make at most 170 wireless headphones per day. Both teams require use of a shared audio testing machine, and this machine can be used to make a maximum of 150 headphones per day. The profit per wired headphone is $50 and the profit per wireless headphone $20. How many of each headphone should be made to maximize profit?

name: Jacob has $3000 to invest
description: Jacob has $3000 to invest. He has decided to invest in his favorite clothing company and his favorite tech company. He has decided that the money invested in his favorite clothing company must be at least four times as much as the amount invested in his favorite tech company. He has also limited himself to invest at most $500 in his favorite tech company. If the money invested in his favorite clothing company earns 7% and the money invested in his favorite tech company earns 10%, how much should he invest in each to maximize his profit?

constraints.description: clothing company at least four of tech company

name: Each month a store owner can spend at most $500 on carrots a...
description: Each month a store owner can spend at most $500 on carrots and cucumbers. A carrot costs the store owner $0.30 and a cucumber costs the store owner $0.50. Each carrot is sold for a profit of $0.75 and each cucumber is sold for a profit of $0.80. The owner estimates that the number of cucumbers sold is at most a third of the number of carrots sold. He also estimates that at least 300 carrots but at most 500 carrots are sold each month. How many of each, carrots and cucumbers, should be sold in order to maximize the profit?

constraints.description: cucumbers at most third of carrots

name: A farmer wants to manufacture a special plant nutrition usin...
description: A farmer wants to manufacture a special plant nutrition using fertilizers A and B. Each kg of fertilizer A contains 13 units of nitrogen, 5 units of phosphoric acid, 6 units of vitamin A and 5 units of vitamin D. Each kg of fertilizer B contains 8 units of nitrogen, 14 units of phosphoric acid, 6 units of vitamin A and 9 units of vitamin D. To be effective, the plant nutrition requires a minimum 220 units of nitrogen, a minimum of 160 units of phosphoric acid, and no more than 350 units of vitamin A. How many kg of each fertilizer should be used to minimize the amount of vitamin D in the nutrition? What is the minimum amount of vitamin D?

name: Mrs
description: Mrs. Watson wants to invest in the real-estate market and has a total budget of at most $760000. She has two choices which include condos and detached houses. Each dollar invested in condos yields a $0.50 profit and each dollar invested in detached houses yields a $1 profit. A minimum of 20% of all money invested must be in condos, and at least $20000 must be in detached houses. Formulate an LP that can be used to maximize total profit earned from Mrs. Watson's investment.

name: A breakfast joint makes two different sandwiches: a regular ...
description: A breakfast joint makes two different sandwiches: a regular and a special. Both need eggs and bacon. Each regular sandwich requires 2 eggs and 3 slices of bacon. Each special sandwich requires 3 eggs and 5 slices of bacon. The joint has a total of 40 eggs and 70 slices of bacon. It makes a profit of $3 per regular sandwich and a profit of $4 per special sandwich. How many of each sandwich should be made to maximize profit?

name: A cleaning company located in Edmonton wants to get the best...
description: A cleaning company located in Edmonton wants to get the best exposure possible for promoting their new dishwashing detergent without exceeding their $250,000 advertising budget. To do so, the company decides to spend their money on two forms of advertising: (1) radio ads and (2) social media ads. Each radio ad costs $5,000; each social media ad costs $9,150. The expected exposure, based on industry ratings, is 60,500 viewers for each radio ad. Additionally, the expected exposure for each social media ad is 50,000 viewers. The company decides that at least 15 but no more than 40 radio ads should be ordered, and that at least 35 social media ads should be contracted. How many ads of each type should be run to obtain maximum exposure while staying within the budget?

name: There is 1000 mg of gold available that is needed to make lo...
description: There is 1000 mg of gold available that is needed to make long and short cables. Long cables require 10 mg of gold while short cables require 7 mg of gold. Because of their compact size, at least 5 times the number of short cables are needed than the long cables. In addition, there needs to be at least 10 long cables made. If each long cable sold results in a $12 profit and each short cable sold results in a $5 profit, how many of each type of cable should be made to maximize profit?

constraints.description: short cables at least 5 of long cables

name: A store employs senior citizens who earn $500 per week and y...
description: A store employs senior citizens who earn $500 per week and young adults who earn $750 per week. The store must keep the weekly wage bill below $30000. On any day, the store requires at least 50 workers, of whom at least 10 must be young adults. To ensure the store runs smoothly, the number of young adults should be at least a third the number of senior citizens. Formulate a LP to minimize the wage bill.

constraints.description: young adults at least third of senior citizens

name: A company is deciding where to promote their product
description: A company is deciding where to promote their product. Some options include z-tube, soorchle engine, and wassa advertisements. The cost for each option and the number of viewers they each attract is given. On z-tube, each ad costs $1000 and attracts 400,000 viewers. On soorchle, each ad costs $200 and attracts 5,000 viewers. On wassa, each ad costs $100 and attracts 3,000 viewers. Soorchle limits the number of advertisements from a single company to fifteen. Moreover, in order to balance the advertising among the three types of media, at most a third of the total number of advertisements should occur on wassa. And at least 5% should occur on z-tube. The weekly advertising budget is $10000. How many advertisements should be run in each of the three types of media to maximize the total audience?

name: A chair produced by Elm Furniture yields a profit of $43, wh...
description: A chair produced by Elm Furniture yields a profit of $43, while every dresser yields a $52 profit. Each week, 17 gallons of stain and 11 lengths of oak wood are available. Each chair requires 1.4 gallons of stain and 2 lengths of oak wood, while each dresser requires 1.1 gallons of stain and 3 lengths of oak wood. Determine the maximum profit.

name: A farmer wants to mix his animal feeds, Feed A and Feed B, i...
description: A farmer wants to mix his animal feeds, Feed A and Feed B, in such a way that the mixture will contain a minimum of 30 units of protein and 50 units of fat. Feed A costs $100 per kilogram and contains 10 units of protein and 8 units of fat. Feed B costs $80 per kilogram and contains 7 units of protein and 15 units of fat. Determine the minimum cost of the mixture.

name: An artisan makes two types of terracotta jars: a thin jar an...
description: An artisan makes two types of terracotta jars: a thin jar and a stubby jar. Each thin jar requires 50 minutes of shaping time and 90 minutes of baking time. Each stubby jar requires 30 minutes of shaping time and 150 minutes of baking time. Per week, there are 3000 minutes available for shaping and 4000 minutes available for baking. The profit per thin jar is $5 and the profit per stubby jar is $9. How many jars of each type should the artisan make to maximize profit?

name: A grocery store wants to liquidate its stock of 10 apples, 2...
description: A grocery store wants to liquidate its stock of 10 apples, 20 bananas, and 80 grapes. Given past experience, the store knows that they can propose a banana-haters package with 6 apples and 30 grapes and that this package will bring a profit of six euros. Similarly, they can prepare a combo package with 5 apples, 6 bananas, and 20 grapes, yielding a profit of seven euros. They know they can sell any quantity of these two packages within the availability of its stock. What quantity of each package, banana-haters packages and combo packages, should the store prepare to maximize net profit?

name: A bakery uses a stand-mixer and a slow bake oven to make bre...
description: A bakery uses a stand-mixer and a slow bake oven to make bread and cookies. Each machine can run for at most 3000 hours per year. To bake a loaf of bread takes 1 hour in the stand mixer and 3 hours in the oven. A batch of cookies requires 0.5 hours in the mixer and 1 hour in the oven. The profit per loaf of bread is $5 and the profit per batch of cookies is $3. How should the bakery operate to maximize total profit?

name: A glass factory makes two types of glass panes: a regular gl...
description: A glass factory makes two types of glass panes: a regular glass pane and a tempered glass pane. Both require time on a heating and cooling machine. Both machines are available for a maximum of 300 minutes per day. It takes 3 minutes in the heating machine and 5 minutes in the cooling machine to make one regular glass pane. It takes 5 minutes in the heating machine and 8 minutes in the cooling machine to make one tempered glass pane. The profit per pane of regular glass is $8 and the profit per pane of tempered glass is $10. How many panes of each glass type should the factory make to maximize profit? What is the maximum profit?

name: A souvenir shop makes wooden elephants and tigers with plast...
description: A souvenir shop makes wooden elephants and tigers with plastic ornaments. Each elephant requires 50 grams of wood and 20 grams of plastic. Each tiger requires 40 grams of wood and 30 grams of plastic. In a week, 5000 grams of wood and 4000 grams of plastic are available. The profit per elephant sold is $5 and the profit per tiger sold is $4. How many of each should be made in order to maximize profit?

name: An art store makes large and small art pieces
description: An art store makes large and small art pieces. The store has available 100 units of paint, 50 units of glitter, and 70 units of glue. To make a large art piece requires 4 units of paint, 3 units of glitter, and 5 units of glue. To make a small art piece requires 2 units of paint, 1 unit of glitter, and 2 units of glue. The store must make at least 5 units of each large and small art pieces. If the profit per large art piece is $30 and the profit per small art piece is $15, how many of each should be made to maximize profit?

name: My family has decided to invest in real state for the first ...
description: My family has decided to invest in real state for the first time. Currently, they have $600,000 to invest, some in apartments and the rest in townhouses. The money invested in apartments must not be greater than $200,000. They have decided that the money invested in apartments must be at least a half as much as that in townhouses.  If the apartments earn 10%, and the townhouses earn 15%, how much money should they invest in each to maximize profit?

constraints.description: apartments at least half of townhouses

name: My grandma is required to take two medicines Z1 and D3 every...
description: My grandma is required to take two medicines Z1 and D3 everyday. She needs to take at least 5 grams of Z1 and 10 grams of D3 everyday. These medicines are available in two pills named Zodiac and Sunny. One pill of Zodiac contains 1.3 grams of Z1 while one pill of Sunny contains 1.2 grams of Z1. On the other hand, one pill of Zodiac contains 1.5 grams of D3 and one pill of Sunny contains 5 grams of D3. The cost per pill of Zodiac is $1 and the cost per pill of Sunny is $3. Formulate a LP such that the medicine requirement can be fulfilled at the lowest cost.

name: A hobbyist makes model trains and planes using wood and pain...
description: A hobbyist makes model trains and planes using wood and paint. A model train requires 3 units of wood and 3 units of paint. A model plane requires 4 units of wood and 2 units of paint. The hobbyist has available 120 units of wood and 90 units of paint. If the profit per model train is $8 and the profit per model plane is $10, how many of each should be made to maximize his profit?

name: An electronics store wants to optimize how many phones and l...
description: An electronics store wants to optimize how many phones and laptops are enough to keep in inventory. A phone will earn the store $120 in profits, and a laptop will earn $40. A phone requires 1 sq ft of floor space, whereas a laptop requires 4 sq ft. In total, 400 sq ft of floor space is available. The store stocks only phones and laptops. Corporate has required that at least 80% of all appliances in stock be laptops. Finally, a phone costs $400 for the store, and a laptop, $100. The store wants to spend at most $6000. Formulate an LP that can be used to maximize the store's profit.

name: A candy store mixes regular candy and sour candy to prepare ...
description: A candy store mixes regular candy and sour candy to prepare two products, regular mix and sour surprise mix. Each kilogram of the regular mix contains 0.8 kg of regular candy and 0.2 kg of sour candy. The profit per kilogram of the regular mix is $3. Each kilogram of the sour surprise mix contains 0.1 kg of regular candy and 0.9 kg of sour candy. The profit per kilogram of the sour surprise mix is $5. The candy store has 80 kg of regular candy and 60 kg of sour candy available. How many kilograms of each type of candy mix should be created to maximize profits?

name: A suspicious factory has 100 sq
description: A suspicious factory has 100 sq. feet of space. It makes bootleg phones and laptops. Phones require 2 hours of labor and cost $12 for each sq. foot of space allocated for phone production (cost of electricity and equipment). Laptops require 3 hours of labor and cost $15 for each sq. foot of space allocated for laptop production. Phones produce a net revenue of $50 per sq. foot while laptops produce a net revenue of $70 per sq. foot. The factory wants to spend at most $5000 and 2000 hours of labor. What is the optimal factory layout to maximize revenue?

name: A electronics store sells premium desktops with more power a...
description: A electronics store sells premium desktops with more power as well as regular desktops for everyday use. Each premium desktop costs the store $2000 to make and yields a profit of $500. Each regular desktop costs the store $1000 to make and yields a profit of $300. The store sells at most 200 desktops each month and wants to spend at most $300000 on making the desktops. How many of each should be made and sold to maximize profit?

name: A car manufacturer makes two versions of the same car, a reg...
description: A car manufacturer makes two versions of the same car, a regular model and a premium model. They make x1 regular models per day and x2 premium models per day. The profit per regular model is $5000 and the profit per premium model is $8500 (x1 and x2 are unknown values both greater than or equal to 0). The daily demand for these cars is limited to and most 8 regular models and 6 premium models. In addition, the manufacturer can make a maximum of 12 cars of either type per day. How many cars of each model should the manufacturer make in order to maximize profit?

name: You are designing an office space with two types of desks: l...
description: You are designing an office space with two types of desks: long desks and short desks. You can spend at most $2000. Long desks cost $300, take up 10 square feet of space, and seat 6 employees. Short desks cost $100, take up 4 square feet of space, and seat 2 employees. The office can have at most 200 square feet of desks. How many of each desk should you buy in order to maximize the seating availability?

name: Ayse produces a plant growth compound by mixing two types of...
description: Ayse produces a plant growth compound by mixing two types of fertilizer: C and Y. This growth compound must contain at least 5 units of nitrous oxide and 8 units of vitamin mix. Fertilizer C and Y cost $2 and $3 per kg respectively. Fertilizer C contains 1.5 units of nitrous oxide per kg and 3 units of vitamin mix per kg. Fertilizer Y contains 5 units of nitrous oxide per kg and 1 unit of vitamin mix per kg. Determine the minimum cost of Ayse's compound.

name: Sleep inducing medicine and anti-inflammatory medicine is fo...
description: Sleep inducing medicine and anti-inflammatory medicine is found in two pills, pill A and pill B. One pill A contains 3 units of sleep inducing medicine and 5 units of anti-inflammatory medicine. One pill B contains 6 units of sleep-inducing medicine and 1 unit of anti-inflammatory medicine. The cost per pill for pill A is $4 and the cost per pill for pill B is $5. A patient must consume these two pills to get at least 40 units of sleep-inducing medicine and 50 units of anti-inflammatory medicine. Formulate a LP to minimize the cost for the patient.

name: A car manufacturer makes two types of car oils: Oil Max and ...
description: A car manufacturer makes two types of car oils: Oil Max and Oil Max Pro. A container of Oil Max contains 46 grams of substance A, 43 grams of substance B and 56 grams of substance C. A container of Oil Max Pro contains 13 grams of substance A, 4 grams of substance B and 45 grams of substance C. The car manufacturer has 1345 grams of substance A, 346 grams of substance B, 1643 grams of substance C. In addition, the profit per container of Oil Max is $10 and the profit per container of Oil Max Pro is $15. How many containers of each of oil should the car manufacturer make to maximize profit?

name: A food truck owner can spend at most $20000 on mangos and gu...
description: A food truck owner can spend at most $20000 on mangos and guavas. A mango costs the food truck owner $5 and a guava costs him $3. Spices are added and each mango is sold for a profit of $3 while each guava is sold for a profit of $4. The owner estimates that at least 100 mangos but at the most 150 are sold each month. He also estimates that the number of guavas sold is at most a third of the mangos sold. How many mangos and guavas should be sold in order to maximize the profit?

constraints.description: guavas at most third of mangos

name: A nutritionist is making a special vitamin mix using two dri...
description: A nutritionist is making a special vitamin mix using two drinks: A and B. The vitamin mix must contain at least 150 units of Vitamin A, at least 300 units of vitamin D, and at most 400 units of vitamin E. A cup of drink A contains 8 units of Vitamin A, 6 units of Vitamin D, 10 units of Vitamin E, and 4 units of Vitamin K. A cup of drink B contains 15 units of Vitamin A, 2 units of Vitamin D, 20 units of Vitamin E, and 12 units of Vitamin K. How many cups of each drink should be used to make the vitamin mix in order to minimize the amount of Vitamin K?

name: An ice cream store makes chocolate and vanilla ice cream by ...
description: An ice cream store makes chocolate and vanilla ice cream by the gallon. In a week, they must make at least 5 gallons of each type but at most 10 gallons of chocolate ice cream and at most 8 gallons of vanilla ice cream. It takes 1 hour to produce a gallon of chocolate ice cream and 2 hours to produce a gallon of vanilla ice cream. In a week, 30 hours are available to make ice cream. In addition at least 6 workers are needed with 1 working on the chocolate ice cream and 2 on the vanilla ice cream at any time. If the profit per gallon of chocolate ice cream is $200 and the profit per gallon of vanilla ice cream is $300, how many gallons of each should be made to maximize profit?

name: Mark has 50 acres of land available to grow potatoes and cuc...
description: Mark has 50 acres of land available to grow potatoes and cucumbers that he sells at a farmers' market. He must grow at least 12 acres of potatoes and 15 acres of cucumbers to meet his contract. Mark prefers to grow more cucumbers than potatoes, but he only has enough resources to grow at most twice the amount of cucumbers as potatoes. If the profit per acre of potatoes is $500 and the profit per acre of cucumbers is $650, how many acres of each should he grow to maximize his profit? What is that profit?

constraints.description: cucumbers at most twice of potatoes

name: A flooring company produces engineered hardwood and vinyl pl...
description: A flooring company produces engineered hardwood and vinyl planks. Their sales forecasts show an expected demand of at least 20,000 square foot of hardwood and 10,000 square feet of vinyl planks each week. To satisfy a shipping contract, a total of at least 60,000 square feet of flooring much be shipped each week. Due to a labor shortage issue, no more than 50,000 square feet of hardwood and 30,000  square feet of vinyl  can be produced weekly. If a square foot of hardwood flooring yields a profit of $2.5 and a square foot of vinyl planks produces a $3 profit, how many of each type of flooring should be made weekly to maximize the company's profit?

name: A berry farmer has two farms, an old and new farm, where he ...
description: A berry farmer has two farms, an old and new farm, where he grows raspberries, blueberries, and strawberries. He has a contract to provide a local store with 10 kg of raspberries, 9 kg of blueberries, and 15 kg of strawberries. At his old farm, it cost $300 to operate per day and he can harvest and deliver 2 kg of raspberries, 2 kg of blueberries, and 4 kg of strawberries in a day. At his new farm, it costs $200 to operate per day and he can harvest and deliver 4 kg of raspberries, 1 kg of blueberries, and 2 kg of strawberries in a day. Formulate a LP to meet his contract while minimizing his cost.

name: Jordan is a chef
description: Jordan is a chef. He wants to design a diet consisting of Kebabs and Rice.  Assume that each serving of Rice costs $3 and contains 300 calories and 4.5 grams of protein. Assume that each serving of Kebab costs $2 and contains 200 calories and 4 grams of protein. He's interested in spending as little money as possible but he wants to ensure that his meals have at least 2200 calories and at least 30 grams of protein per day. Formulate a linear programming problem that will help minimize the cost of the diet.

name: A company sells custom scooters and bikes for customers
description: A company sells custom scooters and bikes for customers. The profit per scooter is $200 and the profit per bike is $300. Each product requires time with the design team and engineering team. Each scooter needs 2 hours with the design team and 3 hours with the engineering team. Each bike needs 4 hours with the design team and 5 hours with the engineering team. Per month, there are 5000 hours available on the design team and 6000 hours available on the engineering team. How many of each should the company make per month to maximize profit?

name: John has a 300 acre berry farm on which to plant blueberries...
description: John has a 300 acre berry farm on which to plant blueberries and raspberries. John has $10000 to spend on watering and 575 days worth of labor available.
